refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall analyze the worksite area by identifying terrain features, detecting obstacles, and representing the work-zone accurately.
refined_fr.rationale: Breaking down the broad requirement into specific sub-functions ensures clarity and targeted verification for terrain analysis, obstacle detection, and work-zone representation, which are critical for safety and operational planning.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Verify through simulation and field tests that the system correctly identifies terrain types, detects obstacles, and generates an accurate work-zone map consistent with ground truth data.
sub_frs: FR1: Analyze terrain features of the worksite area.
FR2: Detect obstacles within the worksite area.
FR3: Represent the work-zone area accurately in the system model.

candidate_dps: DP1: Use LIDAR and camera sensor fusion for terrain and obstacle analysis.
DP2: Implement a geospatial data processing algorithm for work-zone representation.
DP3: Integrate real-time data streaming and processing for dynamic mapping.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1: Use LIDAR and camera sensor fusion for terrain and obstacle analysis.

potential_sources: Data dependency between terrain and obstacle detection algorithms.
Interface coupling with real-time data feeds.
coupling_check.notes: The selected DP logically separates terrain and obstacle analysis but shares sensor data inputs, which is managed via modular data processing pipelines to minimize coupling.

assumptions_risks: Assumes availability and reliability of LIDAR and camera sensors in all environmental conditions.
Risk of sensor data inaccuracies affecting mapping quality.
High safety criticality demands robust fault detection and fallback mechanisms.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider adding redundancy in sensor data sources and error handling to mitigate risks.

im_boundary.name: Vehicle
im_boundary.scope: Physical sensor suite and onboard processing units responsible for terrain and obstacle data acquisition and initial analysis.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Streams processed terrain and obstacle data to NGHE Solution for further mapping and planning.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Supports data exchange between Vehicle and NGHE Solution for real-time updates and command reception.

module_containment_scores.notes: High containment as sensors and initial processing are physically located on the vehicle; low diffusion due to limited external dependencies.
Receives processed data but does not perform initial sensor fusion; moderate containment and diffusion.
Minimal involvement in terrain and obstacle analysis; mostly a consumer of processed data.
Uses mapping data for safety alerts but does not perform analysis.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Assigning terrain and obstacle analysis DP to Vehicle module maximizes physical containment of sensors and processing, reducing interface complexity and improving data integrity.
containment_risks: Potential overload of Vehicle processing resources.
Dependency on Vehicle hardware reliability for critical safety functions.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Evaluate processing load on Vehicle and consider offloading some analysis to NGHE Solution if needed.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The selected DP logically satisfies the refined FRs with clear mapping and manageable coupling.

diffusion_risks: Data streaming over Network Link may introduce latency affecting real-time mapping.
Potential single point of failure in Vehicle processing units.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Good containment within Vehicle module; interfaces are well-defined and limited.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification can be performed via integrated system tests using sensor data and simulated worksite scenarios.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of functions to Vehicle module with defined interfaces to NGHE Solution.

recommended_revisions: Monitor Vehicle processing load and consider modular scaling.
Plan for redundancy in sensor data acquisition and processing.

decision.justification: While the decomposition and assignments are sound, risks related to processing load and sensor reliability require revisiting DP and IM allocations to ensure safety and performance.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Analyze terrain features of the worksite area.
FR2: Detect obstacles within the worksite area.
FR3: Represent the work-zone area accurately in the system model.

revise.why: To address processing load risks and improve fault tolerance by possibly redistributing analysis functions or enhancing module capabilities.

assumptions: Sensors provide reliable data under all operational conditions.
Vehicle has sufficient processing capacity for initial analysis.
open_risks: Sensor data inaccuracies impacting safety-critical mapping.
evidence_needed: Performance benchmarks of Vehicle processing units.
Sensor reliability data under varied environmental conditions.
